description: classifyComplexity uses wordPattern.FindAllString + per-word strings.ToLower, allocating 55 times per call. Pre-compile word extraction as a scanner, pre-lowercase the input once, and use a single-pass keyword counter. Baseline: 24μs, 2.5KB, 55 allocs.

description: classifyFailure does strings.ToLower on full content then searches ~16 patterns in two passes. Called every turn with 10K+ char bash output. Pre-lowercase once, use a compiled multi-pattern matcher (Aho-Corasick or similar). No baseline yet — benchmark first.
title: Skaffen: optimize classifyFailure — O(m*n) substring search called every agent turn

description: estimateMessageTokens iterates ALL messages in context (50-500+) calling h.Count() repeatedly. Could cache token counts for unchanged messages and only recount new ones. No baseline yet.
title: Skaffen: optimize estimateMessageTokens — iterates all messages every turn

description: extractFileActivity unmarshals JSON for EVERY tool call regardless of type. Pre-filter by tool name before unmarshaling to avoid wasted allocations. No baseline yet.
title: Skaffen: optimize extractFileActivity — JSON unmarshal for every tool call

description: PageRank power iteration runs before every agent session. 6 allocations per call, map iteration for dangling node redistribution. Pre-sort node lists, pool rank arrays, early convergence termination. No baseline yet.
title: Skaffen: benchmark and optimize Graph.Rank() PageRank — O(nodes*edges*100) per session

description: ScoreMessages + TopK runs O(n log n) full sort when only top-K are needed. Use a min-heap for O(n log k) selection. Runs during session compaction on 50-500+ messages. No baseline yet.
title: Skaffen: optimize ScoreMessages + TopK — full sort for partial selection during compaction

description: Evaluator.Evaluate runs filepath.Match in a linear scan over all learned overrides for EVERY tool call. With 50+ overrides this is O(n) glob matching per tool invocation. Pre-compile patterns or use a trie. No baseline yet.
title: Skaffen: optimize trust.Evaluator.Evaluate — linear glob scan per tool call

description: convertToolDefs creates a new slice and copies every tool definition each turn (50-200+ tools). Tools are static after loop init — cache once and invalidate only on tool registry change. No baseline yet.
title: Skaffen: cache convertToolDefs — rebuilds tool list every turn unchanged

description: Hub.Broadcast iterates all WebSocket connections O(n*m) creating context per write. Hub.snapshot holds RLock and builds new slice every call. Pool snapshots and batch writes. No baseline yet.
title: Intermute: benchmark Hub.Broadcast + snapshot — event fan-out to 100s of connections

description: ReconcileProject walks spec/task directories, parses YAML and computes SHA256 for every file. For 100+ specs this is heavy. Cache hashes, skip unchanged files by mtime, batch YAML parsing. No baseline yet.
title: Autarch: benchmark ReconcileProject — full filesystem walk with YAML parse + SHA256 per file

description: scoreAllPairs allocates a fresh []scoredPair each call (pre-sized but still GC pressure). At 50×15 scale this becomes the bottleneck. Use sync.Pool or caller-provided buffer. title: Intercore: reduce scoring Assign allocations (95μs/33 allocs at 20×8 — pre-allocate pair slices)

description: AtomicWriteFile creates temp file, writes, syncs, renames with multiple chmod/sync/close calls + directory fsync. Profile syscall overhead at various file sizes (1K-1M). No baseline yet.

description:
Interrank MCP tools (recommend_benchmarks, compare_models, domain_leaderboard) should be able to factor in local hardware constraints when recommending models.

Use case: Agent asks 'what's the best coding model I can run on my RTX 4090?' — interrank should combine AgMoBench domain scores with the local-calc VRAM estimation + quantization quality degradation to recommend the best model that actually fits.

Implementation approach:
- Add optional 'hardware' parameter to recommend tools (gpu_id or vram_gb)
- Reuse agmodb's local-calc logic: GPU database, VRAM formula, MoE active params, quality retention multipliers
- Filter to open-weight models only when hardware constraint is specified
- Return adjusted scores (accounting for quantization degradation) in recommendations
- Could expose as new MCP tool: recommend_local_models(gpu_id, use_case) → ranked models with quant level + adjusted scores

Dependencies:
- agmodb local-calc (shipped: schema, GPU data, VRAM calc, quality retention)
- interrank MCP tools (existing: recommend_benchmarks, domain_leaderboard)
- agmodb snapshot export (needs: isOpenWeight, parametersBillions, activeParametersBillions, isMoe fields)

Origin: agmodb local-calc feature — natural extension for agent-driven model selection.
title: Interrank: add local hardware-aware model recommendation

description: Autoresearch campaign to reduce priompt Render() latency (masaq/priompt/priompt.go). Metric: ns_per_render (nanoseconds, lower_is_better). Levers: batch token counting (CharHeuristic called per-element), pre-compute stable element tokens, cache across turns. Called every LLM turn — 2x speedup saves latency across all Skaffen sessions. Benchmark: BenchmarkRender in priompt_test.go with 20/50/100 element sets.
title: Campaign: priompt-render-latency — optimize prompt compilation speed

description: Autoresearch campaign to improve masaq/diff/diff.go LCS algorithm. Metric: ns_per_diff (nanoseconds, lower_is_better). Current: O(n*m) line-based LCS with full DP table. Levers: switch to Myers diff O(nd), hash-based line comparison, skip Chroma highlighting for unchanged lines. Benchmark: BenchmarkLCS with 100/500/2000-line files at varying change ratios (1%, 5%, 10%).
title: Campaign: diff-lcs-performance — optimize diff computation for large files

description: Autoresearch campaign to reduce MCP discovery latency (os/Skaffen/internal/mcp/manager.go). Metric: ms_per_discovery (milliseconds, lower_is_better). Levers: batch discovery across servers, cache tool metadata across sessions, lazy-load servers on first tool call. Benchmark: measure NewClient()+ListTools() across 5/10/20 MCP servers. Directly impacts session startup latency.
title: Campaign: mcp-discovery-latency — reduce MCP server startup time

description:
When the daemon restarts (or a container session ends for any reason), the agent loses all live conversation context. The next session starts fresh. We need a mechanism to resume conversations efficiently without replaying entire conversation histories into the context window.

Current state:
- Conversations are logged to conversations/ folder (full markdown logs)
- CLAUDE.md is loaded every session (persistent instructions)
- No structured "session handoff" mechanism exists

Requirements:
- Must be token-efficient (can't just dump full conversation history into context)
- Should preserve enough context to continue mid-task work
- Should handle both planned restarts (e.g., after code changes) and unexpected session drops
- Should integrate with the existing memory/file system

Ideas to explore:
- Pre-restart "handoff note" — a structured summary written before triggering restart (what we were doing, what's left, key decisions made)
- Automatic conversation summarization on session end
- A "session state" file that captures active tasks, recent decisions, and pending work
- Tagging system so the next session knows to look for a handoff note
- Using the todo list / beads as implicit session state (already partially works)
- Compact structured format vs prose summaries for token efficiency
title: Design token-efficient conversation resumption across container sessions

description:
Systematic closure of feature gaps identified in the coding agent feature matrix (docs/research/coding-agent-feature-matrix.md). Prioritized by how many competitors already ship the feature. Updated 2026-03-12 after deep research refresh of all 5 competitors.

## Critical Gaps (4+ competitors have, Skaffen doesn't)
- Hook system — CC (17), Codex (5), Gemini (11), OpenCode (28), Amp (2)
- Context compaction — CC, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode (manual + auto)
- Skills/instruction files — CC (CLAUDE.md), Codex (AGENTS.md), Gemini, OpenCode, Amp
- Subagents — CC (5+ built-in), Codex (6 threads), Gemini (4+), OpenCode (4+), Amp (6)
- External editor — CC, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode, Amp (Ctrl+G or similar)
- Shell escape (! prefix) — CC, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode, Amp
- Sandbox/isolation — CC (Seatbelt/bwrap), Codex (4 platforms), Gemini (4 backends)
- Image support — CC, Codex, Gemini, Amp; OpenCode partial
- Web search tool — CC, Codex, Gemini, Amp
- Skills system (SKILL.md) — CC, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode, Amp

## High Priority (2-3 competitors have)
- Custom slash commands — CC, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode
- Vim mode — CC (full), Gemini (/vim)
- History search (Ctrl+R) — CC, Gemini, Amp
- Session forking — CC, Codex, OpenCode
- Per-project config — CC, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode, Amp
- VS Code extension — CC, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode
- Plan mode — CC, Codex, Gemini, Amp
- PR review tools — CC, Codex, Amp
- Custom keybindings — CC, Gemini, OpenCode

## Medium Priority
- Split panes / sidebar (OpenCode)
- Cloud sync (Amp, CC web, Codex cloud)
- Desktop app (CC, Codex, OpenCode)
- Memory system (CC, Codex, Amp)
- Secret redaction (Gemini, Amp)
- Auto-formatting (OpenCode)
- Session export (CC, OpenCode)

## Low Priority
- Agent teams (CC only)
- Browser agent (Gemini only)
- A2A protocol (Gemini only)
- Image generation (Codex, Amp)

## Reference
- Feature matrix: docs/research/coding-agent-feature-matrix.md
- Deep-dive docs: docs/research/2026-03-12-*-feature-inventory.md
title: Bridge competitive landscape gaps in Skaffen TUI

description: Refactor Skaffen internals to split the universal agent loop (provider abstraction, tool registry, model routing, budget tracking, evidence emission, session persistence, MCP integration) from the OODARC workflow engine (phase FSM, phase-gated tool access, phase-aware routing). The agent loop becomes a library that other consumers (Intercom, Autarch) can import directly without adopting the phased workflow. OODARC becomes one workflow built on top of the library. Key changes: Router.SelectModel(phase) → SelectModel(hints SelectionHints) with phase as optional hint; tool registry supports flat registration without phase gates; agent loop is Decide→Act core, not hardcoded OODARC stages. Prerequisite for Intercom Go rewrite (Demarch-mvy) — without this, Intercom would have to force all messages through a 5-phase pipeline.
title: Skaffen: separate agent loop library from OODARC workflow engine

description:
Multi-agent analysis of the full Demarch monorepo (54 plugins, 6 pillars, 3 layers) to identify consolidation, unbundling, and kernel expansion opportunities.

## Key Findings

### Kernel Expansion (push Clavain bash → Intercore Go APIs)
- Phase FSM: 1,717 lines of lib-sprint.sh encode state machine in bash; should be ic phase/fsm.go
- Event pipeline: 3 separate event systems (Intercore, Skaffen JSONL, Intermute domain events) → unified stream
- Routing: Skaffen hardcodes defaults instead of always calling ic route

### Plugin Ecosystem (surprisingly well-scoped)
- Only 1 real consolidation candidate: tldr-swinton ↔ intermap shared Python backend
- Documentation cluster (interdoc/interscribe/interwatch/interlore) could share discovery utils
- 183 auto-generated fd-* agents need audit → ~30 curated

### Duplication & Latency
- lib-verdict.sh duplicated between Clavain and intersynth (130 lines)
- 16 SessionStart hooks, 9 PostToolUse hooks → batch telemetry trio
- Hook saturation is a bigger issue than code duplication

### Wiring Gaps
- Ockham has no runtime integration (intent/authority not wired to dispatch)
- Zaka/Alwe invisible to Intercore (observe via external CASS only)
- Interbase SDK underutilized (only intermem uses it)

## Esoteric Review Agents Generated
7 agents using information theory, economics, ecology, physics, game theory metaphors.
Specs at .claude/flux-gen-specs/esoteric-cross-cutting-analysis.json
title: Monorepo consolidation & kernel expansion — architecture analysis

description:
Run Conway's Game of Life simulation behind the TUI, using true-color (24-bit RGB) background colors to paint living cells in whitespace areas. Particles pulse faintly behind chat content.

## Design
- Game of Life grid matches terminal dimensions (WindowSizeMsg)
- Living cells rendered as dim background colors in empty/whitespace cells
- Dead cells use normal background
- Composited at render time in app.View() — life grid produces bg-color map
- True color (24-bit) for smooth dimming, graceful degradation to 256-color

## Children
- F1: Game of Life engine (grid, step, wrapping, seed patterns)
- F2: Background compositor layer in app.View()
- F3: Easter egg trigger (secret key combo or /life command)
- F4: Terminal capability detection (true color vs 256 vs basic)
- F5: Performance tuning (tick rate, skip when scrolling)

## Constraints
- Must not degrade TUI responsiveness
- Works over SSH + tmux with true color overrides
- Graceful fallback: disable on terminals without true color
- Warp terminal: best-effort (detect TERM_PROGRAM=WarpTerminal)
title: Easter egg: Conway's Game of Life background layer

description:
Add a new IPC task type that allows the main group agent to trigger a graceful restart of intercomd. The daemon picks up a {"type": "restart_service"} JSON file, sends itself SIGTERM, and systemd auto-restarts it.

Requirements:
- New IPC task variant in Rust code
- Main group only (security check)
- Rate limiting to prevent restart loops
- Audit logging
- Graceful shutdown (drain queue before stopping)
- Follow the same pattern as refresh_groups IPC task
notes:
Already fully implemented! The restart_service IPC task type exists end-to-end:

• Rust enum variant: IpcTask::RestartService in intercom-core/src/ipc.rs
• Handler with SIGTERM + rate limiting (60s): intercomd/src/ipc.rs handle_restart_service()
• Authorization: main-group-only check in authorize_task()
• Container-side function: ipc-tools.ts restartService()
• MCP tool: restart_service registered in ipc-mcp-stdio.ts
• Available as mcp__intercom__restart_service in agent sessions

No code changes needed.
title: Add restart_service IPC task type for self-restart

description:
No existing tool does interlab-style autoresearch (edit code → benchmark → keep/discard → repeat) for MoE inference optimization. The space is mostly manual tuning or one-shot benchmarking.

Idea: An autoresearch loop that systematically explores quantization levels × expert placement strategies × offloading ratios per model. Could be an /interlab:autoresearch campaign.

Closest existing work:
- KTransformers (github.com/kvcache-ai/ktransformers) — runtime-adaptive expert placement, not research-loop
- DALI / SpecMoEOff — academic hyperparameter optimizers, not open-sourced as iterative tools
- awesome-moe-inference (github.com/MoE-Inf/awesome-moe-inference) — paper list
- Awesome-Efficient-LLM MoE section (github.com/horseee/Awesome-Efficient-LLM)
- Survey on MoE Inference Optimization (arxiv.org/html/2412.14219v2)

Origin: agmodb local-calc brainstorm — discovered gap while researching flash-moe for expert offloading toggle.
title: Autoresearch for MoE inference optimization
